rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PENNY OLIVIA BEACHUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Petition To Terminate Conservatorship Due To The Death Of The Conservatee On Waiver Of Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER'S CONCEPCION FERRER'S Motion for Reconsideration of Order Granting Motion for Determination of Heirship of the Estate of Mary E. Ferrer a.k.a.. Mary Ernestine Ferrer pursuant to CCP § 1008 is hereby tentatively denied. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER fails to meet her burden of demonstrating the motion is based on new or different law or facts, and that she could not, with reasonable diligence, have discovered and produced the new or different matter at an earlier stage in the proceedings. Claimant filed her Petition for Letters of Administration on August, 2011, where she alleges her husband, Joseph Norberto Ferrer (Barrera) is the brother of the Decedent by virtue of both the Decedent and her husband, Joseph Norberto Ferrer, are adopted by, Ernestina Reynoso Ferrer, the paternal grandmother of Decedent. Respondent, CARLOS FERRER filed a Petition for Revocation of Letters of Administration on January 12, 2012 denying there were any adoptions. The overriding dispute in this matter has always been the same, i.e. whether the Decedent and Joseph Norberto Ferrer had been adopted by the paternal grandmother, Ernestona Reynoso Ferrer. Claimant was on notice of the dispute of heirship from the very beginning. On March 7, 2012, Barbara DeVries was issued Letters of Administration and appointed successor administrator. Successor Administrator Barbara DeVries hired Davis & Brandenburg, a genealogical firm, and on August 13, 2012, sent a letter to all beneficiaries, advising them of the genealogical search and requesting their cooperation. Genealogist Davis researched the heirship matter for more than 8 months. The Motion to Determine Heirship was filed on May 10, 2013. The Petition alleges that Decedent was raised by paternal grandmother, Ernestina Reynoso Ferrer, but no formal adoption took place. Further the Petition alleges that Joseph Norberto Ferrer was the first cousin of Ernestina Reynoso Ferrer, and lived with Ernestina Reynoso Ferrer. DeVries and professional investigative agency, Brandenburg & Davis, were never able to locate definitive proof that the paternal grandmother ever formally adopted the decedent or Joseph Norberto Ferrer. The Motion to Determine Heirship set forth the reasons why Joseph Norbert Ferrer was not an heir. The hearing was set for July 31, 2013. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER did not file a response and/or objection to DeVries' Petition to Determine Heirship. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER fails to demonstrate why her "new" facts and/or evidence were not previously produced with reasonable diligence. She was on notice of the dispute of heirship when Respondent Carolos filed the Petition for Revocation of Letters of Administration on January 12, 2012. She was on notice that DeVries, after professional investigative agency, Brandenburg & Davis conducted more than 8 months of research, filed the Motion to Determine Heirship on May 10, 2013 and sets forth the reasons why Joseph Norbert Ferrer was not an heir. DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER'S Motion to Set Aside Order pursuant to CCP § 473 (b) is hereby tentatively denied. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER does not provide any legal support to justify setting aside the court's order under the facts as discussed. To obtain discretionary relief under § 473(b), Claimant bears the burden to demonstrate the requisite surprise, mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Claimant fails to do so. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415 551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 PM on November 25, 2013. If the tentative ruling is opposed, the hearing will be set on Thursday, December 5, 2013 at 2 p.m. in Dept 204. If the tentatively ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party is to prepare the order.
